sourceMovie.title: Raging Bull
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Raging Bull (1980) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Cinderella Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Raging Bull clicked for you, Cinderella Man (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Raging Bull clicked for you, The Smashing Machine (2025) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Raging Bull?
faq.answer: Try Cinderella Man, The Smashing Machine, You Don't Know Jack—each shares DNA with Raging Bull while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Raging Bull?
faq.answer: Raging Bull (1980) is best described as Drama, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Raging Bull worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
